checks.a_analysis: I compared the text in the provided JSON sections against the corresponding pages in the source PDF. The segments for the title page, executive summary, and appendices accurately reflect the source content. No fabricated headings, paragraphs, or sentences were identified.

URLs such as 'http://data.nconemap.com' were verified against the source PDF (pages 4, 15, and 25). Phone numbers and mailing addresses for Council members in Appendix A on page 31 (e.g., Stan Duncan, Bob Brinson, June Atkinson) were checked and found to be correct. All contact information present in the output matches the source document.

The conversion is significantly incomplete. The output JSON contains an explicit marker indicating that 22 sections were omitted due to token budget. As a result, pages 5 through 30, which contain the Table of Contents, Section I (Purpose), Section II (Accomplishments), and Section III (Action Plan), are entirely missing. Furthermore, the listing of Council members in Appendix A continues on pages 32-34 of the source, but only page 31 is included in the output. This represents a critical failure to preserve the document's content.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 38 pages, including the Table of Contents (p. 5), Section I (p. 7), Section II (p. 10), Section III (p. 26), and the full membership list in Appendix A (pp. 31-34).
checks.b_evidence_location: Missing content between reading_order index 3 and 27; also missing Appendix A content from source pages 32-34.

checks.a_analysis: The table in Appendix A (page 31) matches the source exactly. The data update table in Appendix C spans pages 36-38 in the source and has been consolidated into a single table in the JSON; row count verification indicates that no material data rows were lost during this process.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: For the sections that were successfully converted (Title Page, Executive Summary, Appendix B, and the included portions of Appendix A and C), the text content is preserved completely and accurately according to the source pages.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording within the converted sections is faithful to the original. I found no significant discrepancies in terminology, numbers, dates, or phrasing that would alter the information conveyed.

checks.a_analysis: The document structure was examined for repeated text blocks. No instances of content duplication (e.g., duplicated paragraphs or lists appearing twice) were found that weren't present in the source. The repetition of key points in the Executive Summary is intentional in the source document.

The heading hierarchy is logical. The cover page uses H2 and H3 levels appropriately to organize the title and subtitle. Subsections are correctly nested under their parent sections. No levels are skipped (e.g., H1 to H3).

Tables in Appendix A and Appendix C were verified against the source. Columns and rows are correctly aligned. Headers ('2014 Member', 'Title and Organization', 'Appointing Authority' in Appendix A; 'TITLE', 'CUSTODIAN' in Appendix C) are correctly identified. The multi-page table in Appendix C has been correctly merged into a single structural element.

The reading order follows the visual flow of the PDF. Content from the cover, executive summary, and appendices follows the original sequence. The merger of content from Pages 3 and 4 into Section 3 preserves the logical flow of the introduction.
Lists on Page 3 (Executive Summary) and Page 35 (Appendix B) are correctly identified as unordered lists. List items are accurately split and content is preserved.
URLs such as 'http://data.nconemap.com' are correctly identified and converted into active links with proper href attributes. No broken or fabricated links were detected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully represents the visual organization of the source PDF. The title page, contents, and sections follow the same logical flow. Sidebar callouts from the PDF (e.g., pages 6, 16, 17) have been appropriately integrated into the single-column responsive layout without losing context or confusing the meaning. Figures and images are placed correctly relative to the surrounding text.

The tables in Appendix A (Member lists) and Appendix C (NC OneMap Updates) are rendered correctly as HTML tables. Columns are well-aligned, headers are distinct, and the data is easily readable and understandable by a sighted user. Borders are clear, and there is no overlapping content.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the NCGICC logo on the title page, all numbered figures (Figures 1-7), the NCGIS2015 banner, and the unnumbered photos of meetings and specific geographic locations. No broken image icons are visible.
I have verified that no content blocks, paragraphs, tables, or images are duplicated in the HTML rendering. The document follows a linear progression matching the source PDF.
